title: fix: clarify My Models access and update publishers
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Shows one permission-specific My Models empty message instead of mixing private and publishing guidance.
- Hides the duplicate publishing footer until a model exists.
- Replaces `thomashmyceli` (235942848) with `lizzieliza` (101795137) in the community publishing allowlist.
- Adds explicit allow/deny coverage for both IDs.

Neither account currently owns a production community model, so no model rows or migration are affected.

Checks:
- Biome 2.3.10
- Enter typecheck
- Gen community endpoint tests (20)

gist.headline: One signpost, no duplicates
gist.blurb: My Models now gives each visitor one clear next step instead of handing them two different maps. The publishing garden also has its duplicate footer quietly pruned.
gist.summary: Clarified the permission-specific empty state in My Models, hid the duplicate publishing footer until a model exists, and updated the community publishing allowlist from GitHub user thomashmyceli (235942848) to lizzieliza (101795137).
gist.impact: People visiting My Models now see guidance that matches their access level, while lizzieliza can use the community publishing path with explicit allow/deny test coverage for both GitHub IDs.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy garden noticeboard represents the My Models empty state: Polly replaces two confusing overlapping signs with one clear permission-specific sign, while a small publishing gate receives an updated nameplate. A tiny code-plant and flowers reinforce that community models only appear once they have grown.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene in a warm community garden where Polly, a cute pixel art bee mascot with a yellow body, dark brown horizontal stripes, small black legs, two black antennae, black vertical oval eyes, bright pink rectangular cheek blushes, a small happy smile, and small rounded light blue translucent wings, wears a tiny gardener scarf while replacing two overlapping noticeboard signs with one clear sign reading "MY MODELS"; beside her, a small publishing gate has a fresh nameplate and code-plants sprout from flower pots. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel gradient background, chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, subtle CRT glow, emotionally warm.
